problem_summary: Woodfire Westport has built one of the strongest review profiles in its local market with 573 Google ratings at 4.6 stars, yet the current website provides no booking system, no contact form, and no on-site social proof to convert that reputation into direct reservations. Visitors arriving with clear dining intent currently have no action to take beyond browsing, meaning the site is not capturing the commercial value of its strong organic and Google Maps presence.
recommended_solution: A focused conversion sprint to add a reservation or enquiry widget, embed a curated Google reviews section on the homepage, and confirm a prominent click-to-call phone number is in place on mobile. All changes will be implemented within the existing WordPress build without disrupting the current site structure or desktop performance.
scope_of_work: Install and configure a reservation or enquiry widget on the website, such as OpenTable, ResDiary, or a lightweight contact form, providing visitors with a direct on-site pathway from discovery to confirmed booking or enquiry.

Embed a curated Google reviews section or live review widget on the homepage to surface the 4.6-star rating and 573 reviews at the point of visitor decision-making, strengthening on-site trust without requiring users to navigate away.

Audit and implement a prominent click-to-call phone number displayed above the fold on mobile, ensuring every visitor arriving from Google Maps or search has an immediate low-friction contact option available.
expected_outcomes: Adding a direct booking or enquiry pathway should improve the site's ability to convert visitors arriving with dining intent into confirmed reservations or active enquiries.

Surfacing the existing Google review reputation on the homepage is likely to strengthen visitor confidence at the point of decision, reducing reliance on third-party platforms to validate the business.
A confirmed click-to-call number and improved mobile conversion options should reduce the number of mobile visitors who leave the site without taking any action.
next_steps: Schedule a 15-minute call to confirm whether the business takes reservations or operates as walk-in only, and to agree on the preferred booking or enquiry solution before work begins.
risks_if_delayed: Each week without a booking pathway means visitors arriving with genuine dining intent continue to have no direct on-site route to reserve a table, leaving that conversion value uncaptured. The longer the 4.6-star review reputation goes unsurfaced on the website itself, the longer it remains an asset that only benefits third-party platforms rather than the business directly.
